A SUR-REPLY TO THE OPPOSITION MAY BE FILED AND SERVED NO LATER THAN FIVE COURT DAYS PRIOR TO THE NEW HEARING DATE AND COURTESY COPIES MUST BE PROVIDED PURSUANT TO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-12-2014 Continued To Nov-03-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summ Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-01-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - VINCENT OTYANG, TEL. NO. 225-5752, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. NEWTON OLDFATHER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4283 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication - Motion for summary judgment granted. As to the malicious prosecution claim, Defendant met its initial burden by providing evidence that Plaintiff was arrested by an officer in the scope of his employment. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 1-2.) Defendant is immune from liability per Gov. Code secs. 815.2 and 821.6. As to the violation of Civ. Code sec. 52.1 claim, Defendant met its initial burden by providing evidence that the officer had probable cause to arrest plaintiff and the officer did not use threats, intimidation, or coercion during the arrest. (UMF 1-21.) Plaintiff has not provided any admissible evidence in opposition and has therefore not raised a triable issue of material fact. Further, Plaintiff did not file an opposition to Defendant's separate statement in violation of CRC 3.1350(e). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summ Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-10-2014 Continued To Oct-01-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summ Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-04-2014 Continued To Sep-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication is continued to September 23, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 4, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be granted. A sur-reply to the opposition may be filed and served no later than five court days prior to the new hearing date and courtesy copies must be provided pursuant to Local Rule 2.6B. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/eg
